text.title: Foreign Client Kickoff Checklist Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of Foreign Client Kickoff Checklist must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Run every item every cycle. Skipped items require a one-line written reason logged with the cycle artefact.
rule.rationale: silent skips are the dominant checklist-rot mode.

rule.statement: Each item names its source-of-truth (system, dashboard, URL). Items without a named source are deleted as speculation.
rule.rationale: measurement without a defined gauge is not measurement.

rule.statement: Cap the entire pass at its declared time-box. Items that don't fit get split or deferred, never crammed.
rule.rationale: over-budget checklists are abandoned by cycle 4.

rule.statement: Carry forward un-done items with an aging counter. Items older than 3 cycles trigger escalation or removal.

example.title: Anchored application
example.code: Trigger: Foreign-client project kickoff (2 weeks) Artefact: structured output meeting the contract in content/02-output-contract.xml Evidence: ≥1 named source per rule (URL, ticket, transcript_id) Decision: downstream consumer can act without re-deriving reasoning
example.note: Demonstrates the closed loop the methodology creates — trigger → artefact → decision — with auditability at each step.

example.title: Un-anchored application
example.code: Trigger: 'we should do this thing' Artefact: prose paragraph, no schema Evidence: 'industry best practice' Decision: ambiguous — every reader interprets differently
example.note: Each ambiguity above is a failure mode catalogued in content/03-failure-modes.xml. The methodology exists to prevent exactly this shape.
